rules.description: Syntheses should reference at least one contributing source in their sources array
Concept pages should have a short summary for index discoverability

source.description: A processed summary of a raw source (article, paper, book, video, thread, podcast, etc.). Captures key information from the source in structured form with provenance.

local_copy.description: Wikilink to a local copy of the source (e.g. a saved PDF or clipped note)

author.description: Author or creator of the source

book.description: Book title and author if the source is a book

source_type.description: Kind of source material. Free-form — common values include article, paper, book, video, podcast, thread, substack, tweet, website. Not enum-constrained to allow evolving vocabulary.

sources.description: Wikilinks to other source pages that this one references or extends

concept.description: A concept page — defines and frames a specific idea, mechanism, or domain term. May draw from multiple sources. The building blocks of the wiki's knowledge graph.

sources.description: Wikilinks to source pages that contributed to this concept

related.description: Wikilinks to related concept or synthesis pages

examples.summary: A property of an object that signals how it can be used, first described by Gibson and popularised in design by Norman

entity.description: A concrete entity — a person, organisation, product, model, or other named thing. The building blocks of the wiki's knowledge graph alongside concepts. Entities are distinct from concepts: a concept frames an idea or mechanism; an entity is a specific, identifiable thing in the world.

sources.description: Wikilinks to source pages that contributed to this entity page

related.description: Wikilinks to related entity, concept, or synthesis pages

examples.summary: AI safety research lab founded by Dario Amodei and Daniela Amodei

synthesis.description: A synthesis page — connects multiple concepts and sources into a coherent model, framework, or thesis. The highest-value pages in a knowledge wiki. Must reference contributing sources.

sources.description: Wikilinks to source pages this synthesis draws from

concepts.description: Wikilinks to concept pages this synthesis connects

examples.summary: How Norman's affordance model and feedback principles combine to explain intuitive vs confusing interfaces
sources: [[The Design of Everyday Things - Don Norman]]

note.description: Personal notes, journal entries, talk notes, or rough thoughts not drawn from an external URL.

date.description: Date this entry was created or relates to

index.description: The wiki index — a catalog of all pages with links, summaries, and metadata. Updated by the agent on ingest. Used as the primary navigation entry point.
